Daily success ritual. Clean your space. Look around. Look around. You got all this clutter, all these distractions, stuff thrown all over the place. Clean it up. 90% of what the stuff around you is, like, like, like, just go in your closet right now. Go in your closet or go in your car. And I want you to look at it. I want you to not look at it as you. I want you to look at it as some, some other you or something. Look at your closet. I want you to look in your closet and look that I bet you 50% of what's in your closet you had worn in three months, six months. You got stuff in there that's three and four and five years old. Right? Go look in your car now. You got stuff in your car. It's been, it just, it just, you're driving around with clutter, distractions, garbage, the past. What I want you to do today is I want you to clean up your past. I want you to get rid of all the stuff around you that you don't need. I do this every day. I walk in my office. Did I look at this yesterday? Did I need this yesterday? Did I need it last week? If I haven't looked at it in a week, throw it away. I don't need it. If I've somehow survived for the last 30 days and I'm doing well without touching it, throw it away. Why do I need to collect yesterday? I don't need to collect yesterday. I need to collect tomorrow's. And I can't bring in new tomorrow's, new success, new ideas, new friends dragging around the old ones. Look, you got friends that are clutter. You got friends that are distractions. Right? You got people in your phone. Your phone's filled up with numbers. You don't even talk to these people anymore. Delete them. Get rid of them. I don't really mean that. But you know what I'm saying? I'm trying to make a point to you, okay? Because I would never get rid of somebody's name and number because I might want to cold call them tomorrow. You understand? I just, I just came back to my senses. But you got to get clean, get rid of the clutter. You got to get rid of the distractions. You need to get control of your environment. Wake up early, two hours early. Work out, take care of my body, and then I take care of my physical environment. What's my physical environment look like? It's clean. I have control. Control, remember this, control equals income.
